assertions.description: States the 24-hour initial notification obligation to SWIFT (via security@swift.com or KYC-SA portal) upon confirmed cyber incident
assertions.rationale: Response states 'SWIFT expects initial notification within 24 hours of discovering/confirming a cyber incident' and provides 'security@swift.com' as the contact.

assertions.description: States the 30-day full incident report obligation to SWIFT
assertions.rationale: Response states 'A more detailed incident report is typically required within approximately 30 days' covering root cause, impact, and remediation.

assertions.description: Lists evidence to preserve: system memory dumps, log files from SWIFT zone, transaction records, network captures, endpoint forensic images
assertions.rationale: Response provides a comprehensive evidence table including: memory dumps, SWIFT transaction logs, system event logs, network logs, SIEM data, disk images (forensic images), authentication logs, malware files, and network captures (PCAP). All required evidence types are present.

assertions.description: Identifies immediate containment steps: isolate affected systems from SWIFT zone, revoke compromised operator credentials/tokens, notify SWIFT relationship manager
assertions.rationale: Response lists: 'Isolate the affected server(s) from the SWIFT network', 'Revoke credentials of any SWIFT operators who accessed the affected system', and 'Notify your SWIFT Relationship Manager' in the immediate actions section.

assertions.description: Outlines what a SWIFT-specific IRP must contain: detection triggers specific to SWIFT, escalation to SWIFT within 24 hours, fraud scenario playbooks, communication with correspondent banks, evidence preservation procedures
assertions.rationale: Response includes all required IRP elements: SWIFT-specific detection triggers, escalation to SWIFT within 24 hours with security@swift.com, fraud scenario playbooks (unauthorized transaction, compromised credentials, malware on endpoint), communication with correspondent banks, and evidence preservation procedures.
